script: I worked with things you can measure. Data. Models. Dashboards.

props.quote: For most of my adult life, I worked with things you can measure.

script: a poem finds the exact nerve no dataset ever mapped
In a line of code you wrote at 2 AM that reads like confession

lines: A line of code at 2 AM that reads like confession.
The way your mother once described rain.
Grief that arrived when a number told you something true.

script: Poetry was already in the server room. It was never waiting for an invitation.

props.quote: Poetry was already in the server room. It was never waiting for an invitation.

script: The grey of a commute counts. The brown of bad coffee counts.

lines: The grey of a commute counts.
The brown of bad coffee counts.
The particular blue of a Tuesday afternoon.

script: Poetry is not elevated experience. It's ordinary experience, seen clearly for one second.

props.quote: Poetry is not elevated experience. It's ordinary experience, seen clearly for one second.

script: Facts tell you what happened. Poetry tells you what stayed behind after it happened.

props.quote: Facts tell you what happened. Poetry tells you what stayed behind.

script: It dips its fingers in every colour because it's patient.
The door doesn't need to be wide. A crack is enough.

props.quote: The door doesn't need to be wide. A crack is enough.

script: That is not a small thing. It is the whole thing.
one line that followed you home. Bring it. This is Breath of Poetry.

props.quote: One line that followed you home. Bring it.
